issue_title: Incorrect error messages used during failed OIDC client authentication
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

OIDC client authentication uses different error messages in the case when client does not exists and/or in the case when client is disabled and/or in the case when client is enabled, but bad client credentials are used. This allows "client enumeration" and also error messages returned are not 100% aligned with the OAuth2/OIDC specifications, so will be good to fix this IMO .

### Current behaviour

I am sending this request:
```
curl -d "client_id=account1" -d client_secret=wDVOYAWe8R0LxkVGe1MOI5PcuVyWlZ14 -d "username=admin" -d "password=admin" -d "grant_type=password" "http://localhost:8081/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token"
```

1. In case the client `account1` does not exists, the response is:
```
{"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"Invalid client credentials"}
```

2. In case the client `account1` exists, but the client secret is incorrect, the response is:
```
{"error":"unauthorized_client","error_description":"Invalid client secret"}
```

3. In case the client `account1` exists, but is disabled, the response is:
```
{"error":"unauthorized_client","error_description":"Invalid client credentials"}
```

### OAuth2 specification

I've checked the OAuth2 specification https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-5.2 . According to the specification, the error "unauthorized_client" should be returned just when client is not authorized for the specified grant type. It should be never returned when client authentication failed (which is what we are doing right now).

### Fix suggestion

I suggest to change the logic like this:

- client_id parameter missing: Return error "invalid_request" . This is ok per OAuth2 specification. Same applies when request is otherwise malformed (EG. Contains multiple client authentication methods)

For all the other cases below, we can return the generic error to hide the reason:
```
{"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"Invalid client or Invalid client credentials"} 
 ```

However we should differentiate the cases below in the error event, which is thrown to event SPI. The cases are:

- Case when "client_id" points to non-existing client
- Case when "client_secret" is missing as parameter and "Authorization" header is missing as well
- Case when client exists, but is disabled
- Case when client secret is invalid
- Case when client uses different client authentication than the default "Client secret", but invalid client credentials are provided

When refactoring this, we should check that the "framework", which triggers client authentication, checks that client is enabled or not. Right now, I see that there is check in the client authenticator itself - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.0/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/client/ClientIdAndSecretAuthenticator.java#L113-L115 , which is likely not very great as in the case when there is alternative client authentication method and the "Client Authenticator implementation" forget to check the client enablement status, it might be possible to authenticate with disabled client, which is incorrect.

### Version

18.0.0

Incorrect error message OIDC client authentication (#14656)

closes #12162


Co-authored-by: Pedro Hos <pedro-hos@outlook.com>

issue_title: Possible client enumeration in the authorization endpoint
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

One possible place for the client enumeration is the initial request to OIDC authentication endpoint like this
http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=security-admin-console&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fadmin%2Fmaster%2Fconsole%2F&state=f095dd94-529b-40ed-976a-c13732a9b66a&response_mode=fragment&response_type=code&scope=openid&nonce=917cd322-f44e-4928-89e9-bdf874b72af2&code_challenge=4IkfqKxN2HIjcNtOJpUUN7uLo31fgVlR08BjEn7puKg&code_challenge_method=S256

Right now, it displays:

- Error "Client not found" in case that client_id points to non-existing client
- Error "Invalid parameter: redirect_uri" in case that client exists, but redirect_uri parameter is incorrect.

We should probably display same error screen in both cases (But have different error event thrown). Possible attacker will be able to enumerate client just if he knows also his valid redirect URI, which makes the enumeration much harder. We can just always display "Invalid parameter: redirect_uri" as in the case when client pointed by client_id does not exists, it automatically implies that redirect_uri is also incorrect for such a client.

### Version

18.0.0

issue_title: Inconsistent authorization behavior when exporting data from a realm
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

_Originally reported by_ **Muhammad Uwais**

## Scenario 1

An authenticated user with "View Realm" role enabled would be able to export all the information about the realm and disclose all "Client scopes" name and information.

Export and Client Scope feature is only available to the the users which have the role mapping as "Realm Admin" or "Manage-Realm/Manage-Client" enabled.  

### Steps to reproduce

1- Log into keycloak as a non-admin user with "view-realm" role mapping.  
2- Browser to [http://localhost:8080/admin/PentestRealm/console/#/realms/PentestRealm/partial-export](http://localhost:8080/admin/PentestRealm/console/#/realms/PentestRealm/partial-export)
3- Click "Export" and you will be able to export the realm information in json format.

## Scenario 2

An authenticated user with role mapping set to "View-Realm" would be able to determine the number of Groups for the realm.
Groups count is only available to the users with role mapping set as "Realm-Admin" or "Manage-Groups".

### Steps to reproduce

1- Log into the keycloak with the "View-Realm" role mapping.  
2- Make any request and intercept it to get the authentication bearer token. (use any proxy tool such as burp). Copy the Bearer token for the account.
3- Make the following curl request and change the <token> from step 2.
```
curl [http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/PentestRealm/groups/count?top=true](http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/PentestRealm/groups/count?top=true) -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
```

**Recommendation/Fix:** Validate the permission on the mentioned request in POC.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Users with `View Realm` role should not be enabled to export all the information about the realm and all "Client scopes" name and information, or determine the number of groups.

### Actual behavior

Users with `View Realm` role can export all the information about the realm and all "Client scopes" name and information, or determine the number of groups.

### Anything else?

Potential fixes suggested by @JoshuaMulliken:
1. Add documentation or help text that explains that the view-only role allows users to export ALL realm data including the client scope info through the API (I would also recommend showing the export button to the user).
2. Restrict the API endpoint for those with the view-only role to force it to match the UI

issue_title: Jpa map storage: failure to process changelogs when using CockroachDB
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After the addition of new unique constraints for the attributes tables, the changelogs can't be processed anymore when using `CockroachDB` because of lack of support for `DEFERRABLE` and `INITIALLY DEFERRED` options.

The new constraint looks like this:

```
        <addUniqueConstraint tableName="kc_client_scope_attribute" columnNames="fk_root, name, value" deferrable="true" initiallyDeferred="true" />
```

When Keycloak attempts to create the DB schemas using `CockroachDB`, it fails with:

```
Caused by: liquibase.exception.MigrationFailedException: Migration failed for change set META-INF/client-scopes/jpa-client-scopes-changelog-1.xml::client-scopes-1::keycloak:
     Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ERROR: at or near "EOF": syntax error: unimplemented: this syntax
  Detail: source SQL:
ALTER TABLE public.kc_client_scope_attribute ADD UNIQUE (fk_root, name, value) DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED
                                                                                                           ^
  Hint: You have attempted to use a feature that is not yet implemented. See: https://go.crdb.dev/issue-v/31632/v21.2 
```

After reading the issue linked in the error description, I saw that there's no indication that support for these options will be added in the near future, so we might need to split these constraints into a separate changeset targeting `PostgreSQL` only, leaving the constraint out in case `CockroachDB` is used.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The changesets should be processed successfully when using both `PostgreSQL` and `CockroachDB`.

### Actual behavior

Changesets can't be processed when using `CockroachDB` due to unsupported options in the unique constraint found in the attributes tables.

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the instructions at https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9596 to start Keycloak with `CockroachDB` - just attempt to start the server with a clean database.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Deferred indexes are not available on CockroachDB, therefore, only use them on PostgreSQL

Closes #12176

issue_title: ConcurrentModificationException in script cache of JSPolicyProviderFactory
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When (re)starting KeyCloak (KC) and trying to request several REST API resources secured with KC at the same time we often get a ConcurrentModificationException. (PolicyEnforcer is configured in the KC adapter for REST API). As a result a request is rejected with 403 error.

 
The cause is located in the ScriptCache class of the JavaScript authorization policy provider.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/master/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/js/ScriptCache.java

 

This class uses a unsynchronized list as cache

        cache = new LinkedHashMap<String, CacheEntry>(16, DEFAULT_LOAD_FACTOR, true) {
            @Override
            protected boolean removeEldestEntry(Map.Entry eldest) {
                return cache.size()  > maxEntries;
            }
        };

Using the method "parkForWriteAndCheckInterrupt()" before map modifications in ScriptCache doesn't help due to 
any get operation also lead to a map modification **due to LRU** behavior which is implemented via LinkedHashMap.

16.1.1

Absence of ConcurrentModificationException and avoiding unexpected 403 error.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure KC confidential client in which Authorization is turned on. Authorization configuration of KC client should contain JS policies. 
2. Secure REST API with KC client configured on the step 1.
3. (re)start KC. 
4. Request several resources of REST API secured with KC at the same time (usually UI can request several REST API resources at the same time for Views with complex business logic)
5. 403 error may be occurred despite correct credentials. ConcurrentModificationException in the KC logs.

**The issue is reproduced only on JDK 11, it is ok on JDK 8**

### Anything else?

Till now we solve the problem via KC extensions. We implemented SPI which introduces JS Policy implementation with thread safe script cache behavior.

The ScriptCache implementation which solves the problem and is made based on original implementation:

`package authorization.policy.provider;

import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.Map;
import java.util.function.Function;

import org.apache.commons.collections4.map.LRUMap;
import org.keycloak.scripting.EvaluatableScriptAdapter;

public class ScriptCache {

    private final Map<String, CacheEntry> cache;
    private final long                    maxAge;

    /**
     * Creates a new instance.
     *
     * @param maxEntries the maximum number of entries to keep in the cache
     */
    public ScriptCache(int maxEntries) {
        this(maxEntries, -1);
    }

    /**
     * Creates a new instance.
     *
     * @param maxEntries the maximum number of entries to keep in the cache
     * @param maxAge     the time in milliseconds that an entry can stay in the cache. If {@code -1}, entries never expire
     */
    public ScriptCache(final int maxEntries, long maxAge) {
        cache = Collections.synchronizedMap(new LRUMap<>(maxEntries));
        this.maxAge = maxAge;
    }

    /**
     *  Compute if absent.
     */
    public EvaluatableScriptAdapter computeIfAbsent(String id, Function<String, EvaluatableScriptAdapter> function) {
        EvaluatableScriptAdapter adapter = removeIfExpired(cache.get(id));

        if (adapter != null) {
            return adapter;
        }

        CacheEntry entry = cache.computeIfAbsent(id, key -> new CacheEntry(key, function.apply(id), maxAge));

        return entry.value();
    }

    /**
     * Removes by key.
     */
    public void remove(String key) {
        cache.remove(key);
    }

    private EvaluatableScriptAdapter removeIfExpired(CacheEntry cached) {
        if (cached == null) {
            return null;
        }

        if (cached.isExpired()) {
            remove(cached.key());
            return null;
        }

        return cached.value();
    }

    private static final class CacheEntry {

        final String                   key;
        final EvaluatableScriptAdapter value;
        final long                     expiration;

        CacheEntry(String key, EvaluatableScriptAdapter value, long maxAge) {
            this.key = key;
            this.value = value;
            if (maxAge == -1) {
                expiration = -1;
            } else {
                expiration = System.currentTimeMillis() + maxAge;
            }
        }

        String key() {
            return key;
        }

        EvaluatableScriptAdapter value() {
            return value;
        }

        boolean isExpired() {
            return expiration != -1 && System.currentTimeMillis() > expiration;
        }
    }
}`

The issue has been already noticed here https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-17806

issue_title: Dynamic scopes: requested scopes get mixed up between token requests
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We're experimenting with the new dynamic scopes feature and have found it to be a very promising solution for a number of our requirements. However, when we enable dynamic scopes we run into a problem during the authorization code flow and when redeeming refresh token.

If we enable dynamic scopes, the requested scopes seem to be stored inside the client session rather than being stored together with the authorization code. This leads to a problem if a client requests multiple authorization codes in succession before beginning to exchange those tokens because the newest request always overwrites the ClientSessionNotes storing the requested scope. Exchanging an older authorization code for an access token can result in different scopes being included in the AccessToken than originally requested.

This problem also affects refresh tokens. When exchanging a refresh token for a renewed access token, the access token will contain the scopes stored inside the client session note. The resulting new refresh token shows the same scopes as the renewed access token. 

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

AccessTokens and RefreshTokens contain the originally requested scopes even if multiple authorization code or refresh token requests are sent and exchanged.

### Actual behavior

Since the requested scopes are stored inside the client session, the access tokens / refresh tokens always contain the latest requested scopes.



### How to Reproduce?

0. Enable feature "dynamic scopes"
1. Client requests an authorization code for scope A
2. Keycloak returns the requested authorization scope and stores the requested scopes inside the client session
3. Client requests another authorization code for scope B
4. See 2.
5. Client now exchanges the authorization code received in the second step. Since the requested scopes are stored inside the client session, the resulting access token shows scope "B" rather than the expected scope "A".

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Ingress creation loop
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The nightly operator stucks in an ingress creation loop, even when ingress creation is disabled.

First, the operator removes the Ingress when `disableDefaultIngress: true` and existing ingress is found. Then a new ingress is created again. And so on...
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8f54f03f17cbe985e763fb4ce101c569d2842740/operator/app/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/v2alpha1/KeycloakIngress.java#L46-L51

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Ingress should not be created when `disableDefaultIngress: true`

### Actual behavior

Ingress is created, then removed, then created again in a loop manner.

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy nightly operator and apply a Keycloak CRD with `disableDefaultIngress: true`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Client Policies: pkce-enforcer executor with client-access-type condition is not applied on client change via Admin API
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I like to make sure that public and confidential clients can only be created with PKCE configured appropriately (PKCE challenge method should be S256).
For this purpose, I created a Client Policy with a pkce-enforcer executor. Because I don't want to require this pkce-config for bearer-only clients, I used a client-access-type condition. But the pkce-executor is not triggered when creating or updating a (public or confidential) client via the Admin API.



### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

When creating or updating a client via the Admin API, it should be checked whether PKCE is appropriately configured.

### Actual behavior

Clients can be created or updated without or inappropriate PKCE configuration (code challenge method "plain").

### How to Reproduce?

I created a Client Profile named pkce-policy with the following steps:

1. Creating a pkce-profile:
```
curl --request PUT 'https://<host>/auth/admin/realms/<realm_name>/client-policies/profiles' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "profiles": [
        {
            "name": "pkce-profile",
            "executors": [
                {
                    "executor": "pkce-enforcer",
                    "configuration": {
                        "auto-configure": false
                    }
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}'
```

2. Creating the pkce-policy based on the profile, with client-access-type restricted to "confidential" and "public":
```
curl --request PUT 'https://<host>/auth/admin/realms/<realm_name>/client-policies/policies' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "policies": [
        {
            "name": "pkce-policy",
            "enabled": true,
            "conditions": [
                {
                    "condition": "client-access-type",
                    "configuration": {
                        "is-negative-logic": false,
                        "type": [
                            "confidential",
                            "public"
                        ]
                    }
                }
            ],
            "profiles": [
                "pkce-profile"
            ]
        }
    ]
}'
```

Now, when creating a new public client without pkce config, I would expect an error, but the client is successfully created (status 201):
```
curl --request POST 'https://<host>/auth/admin/realms/<realm_name>/clients' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "clientId": "test-public",
    "rootUrl": "https://localhost:8443",
    "adminUrl": "https://localhost:8443",
    "enabled": true,
    "redirectUris": [
        "https://localhost:8443/*"
    ],
    "webOrigins": [
        "https://localhost:8443"
    ],
    "standardFlowEnabled": true,
    "protocol": "openid-connect"
}'
```

When updating a client without pkce config, it also succeeds (status 204). I would also expect an error in this case.
```
curl --request PUT 'https://<host>/auth/admin/realms/<realm_name>/clients/<clientUuid>' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "clientId": "test-public",
    "rootUrl": "https://localhost:8443",
    "adminUrl": "https://localhost:8443",
    "enabled": true,
    "redirectUris": [
        "https://localhost:8443/*"
    ],
    "webOrigins": [
        "https://localhost:8443"
    ],
    "standardFlowEnabled": true,
    "protocol": "openid-connect"
}'
```

Is this a bug, or am I missing something in my configuration?

### Anything else?

It seems like the scenario would work as expected, if:

- either ClientAccessTypeCondition#applyPolicy would actually handle UPDATE and REGISTER (not to confuse with UPDATED and REGISTERED) events - see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7335abaf08915d364f16667733237aa8ac8a3b0f/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientpolicy/condition/ClientAccessTypeCondition.java#L68
- or PKCEEnforcerExecutor#executeOnEvent would actually handle UPDATED and REGISTERED (not to confuse with UPDATE and REGISTER) events - see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientpolicy/executor/PKCEEnforcerExecutor.java#L93
- or both

Not sure what's the best solution in this case - I'm using Client Policies for the first time.
commit_message:
Client Policies: pkce-enforcer executor with client-access-type condition is not applied on client change via Admin API

Closes #12295

issue_title: Deadlock when calling removeUser for Service Account Linked Users
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm with the IT User team at Red Hat. We are working on introducing an API which will allow dynamic creation / deletion of service accounts in one of our realms. The realm is configured with federated user storage. We are frequently noticing the following deadlock during deletion of service accounts:
```
Uncaught server error: javax.persistence.OptimisticLockException: org.hibernate.exception.LockAcquisitionException: could not execute statement
	at org.hibernate@5.3.23.Final-redhat-00001//org.hibernate.internal.ExceptionConverterImpl.wrapLockException(ExceptionConverterImpl.java:277)
	at org.hibernate@5.3.23.Final-redhat-00001//org.hibernate.internal.ExceptionConverterImpl.convert(ExceptionConverterImpl.java:98)
	at org.hibernate@5.3.23.Final-redhat-00001//org.hibernate.internal.ExceptionConverterImpl.convert(ExceptionConverterImpl.java:181)
	at org.hibernate@5.3.23.Final-redhat-00001//org.hibernate.query.internal.AbstractProducedQuery.executeUpdate(AbstractProducedQuery.java:1654)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-model-jpa@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.storage.jpa.JpaUserFederatedStorageProvider.preRemove(JpaUserFederatedStorageProvider.java:826)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-services@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager.removeUser(UserStorageManager.java:252)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-model-infinispan@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.removeUser(UserCacheSession.java:802)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-model-infinispan@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.RealmCacheSession.removeClient(RealmCacheSession.java:601)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-model-infinispan@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.RealmAdapter.removeClient(RealmAdapter.java:809)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-services@15.0.4.redhat-00001//org.keycloak.services.managers.ClientManager.removeClient(ClientManager.java:99)
```

It is failing on the named queries found in `JpaUserFederatedStorageProvider::preRemove`. Called by `UserStorageManager::removeUser`.

It seems these queries are responsible for cleaning up federated user relations during deletion of a user. I think for the internally created service account user, these may not be necessary. And could help prevent deadlocks and improve performance by not querying and locking against these contentious tables.

Let me know what you think.

### Version

RH SSO 7.5

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message: Do not try to delete from related federated user tables when deleting a service account linked user.

issue_title: Security bug: Timing Oracle @ Authorization Grant Request , CWE 208
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

I am doing a pentest for a client of mine. Responsible disclosing the bug wasn't possible. It was just turned down as I wasn't the recent version I was testing this against. 
 
**I** was supposed to prove that this bug also exists in the latest version -- which I can't as it is a pentest and nobody will pay me for this. So this is my swift response on the response I just encountered. I am doing this because I suspect the bug exists also in the latest version and I want software to be secure.

### Details:
During an authorization grant request to a keycloak server I tried to figure out whether one can guess userids by observing the response time. HTTP request to the keycloak server was

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8036727/171483017-e6d06e29-9459-40a7-bf33-643f47361283.png)

The hostname and HTTP ``Host`` header was the keycloak server. ``password`` was just an incorrect arbitrary one.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8036727/171483275-6b63617d-0027-4df5-85ca-905c6db6dc0d.png)

The column payload is the contents of the ``username`` variable above, the column starting with "Respon..." is the Response received time in milliseconds (was performed on a DSL).

If I order the request by response received time one can see that the requests with the existing usernames were responded significantly later (>100 ms) than the on-existing ones (<50ms), thus allowing the identification of usernames.

Usually that's the case when internally the real usernames are checked for their password -- hence the delay and the others just come back when the username isn't valid. It's recommended you add a baseline delay for the latter and some random time on any request.

An attacker can thus use e.g. so called dumps from the internet with just e-mail addresses and guess usernames (which in my case is the same. Otherwise a username dictionary would help). Once the usernames were identified, using a password list (in several projects @ github available) would then give full access. See also https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/208.html .

As said this was discovered through a pentest. Atm I do not have access to the management interface. And probably I don't have access to the system anymore in a few days,

Fingerprinting the version tells me the keycloak version my client is using is between 15.0.2 and 13.0.1 . I haven't looked into the code whether the delay I suggested is implemented on recent versions as I spent a couple of hours already preparing the issue and reporting the bug.

For the sake of security it would be great if you or anybody reading this could check whether the security bug exists in 18.X too and if it does: fix that!

Cheers / thanks,

Dirk

### Version

~15.0.2

### Expected behavior

see above

### Actual behavior

see above

### How to Reproduce?

see above

### Anything else?

I am really scratching my head when people spent time reporting a security bug _why_ they are being turned down with such lame response. This may not shed a good light on the company behind this or the project -- which I normally appreciate.

issue_title: AccessTokens generated from RefreshTokens without scope
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In my scenario, I want to make the scope `roles` Optional for a specific client A.
As consequence, if the client A wants this information, it's necessary inform it in the list of the scopes when you're requesting the `access_token`.

I notices that when you request a new `access_token` from `refresh_token`, the information related to `roles` scope doesn't appears anymore into `access_token`.

I notice this only when 

### Version

15

### Expected behavior

`roles` information appears in the `access_token` when this information is requested even if it was defined as Optional Token.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Part 1:

1. Create a Client;
2. Go to `Client Scopes`;
3. Remove `roles` from "Assigned Default Client Scopes";
4. Include `roles` from into "Assigned Optional Client Scopes".

Part 2:

1. Request a `access_token` without informing `roles`, notice that information related to it will not appears;
1. Request a new `access_token` informing `roles`, notice that information related to it will appears;
1. Request a new `access_token` using from `refresh_token` from the previous request, notice that information related to it will not appears.

### Possible solution

I think it might be good that refresh-token always contains all optional scopes (even those with `Include in access token` is false). This is probably better than using the scope from clientSession as that may not work 100% accurately in case of more browser tabs (that is the limitation of having single authenticatedClientSession per client attached to userSession). See my comment https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12860#issuecomment-1184694501

Closes #12326

issue_title: OAuth2 device polling interval default set to wrong value
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Instead of DEFAULT_OAUTH2_DEVICE_POLLING_INTERVAL, constant for the lifespan was used to initialize the default polling interval.

This leads to inability to continuously poll the result as the result stuck in the actionTokens cache for far longer than expected (600 seconds instead of 5 seconds). As a result, only the first request for the token succeeds if a resource owner already did grant the access. If that has not happened, any additional polling within 600 seconds would get rejected with a 'slow_down' response.

This makes hard to write OAuth 2.0 clients using device code authorization grant flow against multiple IdPs. Microsoft's
implementation of OAuth 2.0 device code grant flow requires 'nudging' the Authorization Server's token endpoint before it even starts recognizing the device code. Keycloak mismatch of the polling interval default makes this flow impossible.

### Version

17.0

### Expected behavior

DEFAULT_OAUTH2_DEVICE_POLLING_INTERVAL should be used as a default for polling interval

### Actual behavior

DEFAULT_OAUTH2_DEVICE_CODE_LIFESPAN is used as the default for the polling interval.


### How to Reproduce?

Create OAuth2 client allowed to perform device authorization flow with default parameters. Request a token on behalf of a resource owner and retry after 5 seconds, as expected. Instead of waiting for the authorization, you'd get 'slow_down' response and will not be able to obtain the token at all because by the time default polling interval ends (600), token lifespan expires too.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
OAuth2DeviceConfig: fix polling interval defaults

Instead of DEFAULT_OAUTH2_DEVICE_POLLING_INTERVAL, constant for the
lifespan was used to initialize the default polling interval.

This leads to inability to continuously poll the result as the result
stuck in the actionTokens cache for far longer than expected (600
seconds instead of 5 seconds). As a result, only the first request for
the token succeeds if a resource owner already did grant the access. If
that has not happened, any additional polling within 600 seconds would
get rejected with a 'slow_down' response.

This makes hard to write OAuth 2.0 clients using device code
authorization grant flow against multiple IdPs. Microsoft's
implementation of OAuth 2.0 device code grant flow requires 'nudging'
the Authorization Server's token endpoint before it even starts
recognizing the device code. Keycloak mismatch of the polling interval
default makes this flow impossible.

Closes #12327

Signed-off-by: Alexander Bokovoy <abokovoy@redhat.com>

issue_title: JPA Map Storage: Keycloak fails to start on CockroachDB
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When any storage is configured to use the Map JPA provider, Keycloak with CockroachDB fails to start due to lack of support for computed columns in foreign keys, which are present in the authorization changesets:

```
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.RuntimeException: RESTEASY003325: Failed to construct public org.keycloak.services.resources.KeycloakApplication()
	at org.jboss.resteasy.core.ConstructorInjectorImpl.construct(ConstructorInjectorImpl.java:164)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.spi.ResteasyProviderFactory.createProviderInstance(ResteasyProviderFactory.java:2835)
...

Caused by: liquibase.exception.LiquibaseException: liquibase.exception.MigrationFailedException: Migration failed for change set META-INF/authz/permission/jpa-authz-permission-changelog-1.xml::authz-permission-1::keycloak:
     Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ERROR: unimplemented: computed column "resourceserverid" cannot reference a foreign key
  Hint: You have attempted to use a feature that is not yet implemented.
See: https://go.crdb.dev/issue-v/46672/v21.2 [Failed SQL: (0) ALTER TABLE public.kc_authz_permission ADD CONSTRAINT authz_permission_fk_root_fkey FOREIGN KEY (resourceserverid) REFERENCES public.kc_authz_resource_server (id) ON DELETE CASCADE]

```

We might have to move all the foreign key constraints in the authz changelogs that reference a computed column to a changeset that runs only for PostgreSQL.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should be able to process all new storage changelogs when CockroachDB is used.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak fails to start if any area is configured to use the new Map JPA store (which in Cockroach's case leads to all changelogs being processed)

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

The following example first looks up the realmId, then looks it up via the session again. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fc974fc01911af06e8431f03a0389a69a0131473/model/map/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/role/MapRoleProvider.java#L194-L200

issue_title: JPA map store uses bulk delete, but keeps entities in the persistence context
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The current bulk delete doesn't clear or flush which is normally used before or after a bulk operation. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/be28e866b9bdfac39cae13d06a010994a1e058a7/model/map-jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/jpa/JpaMapKeycloakTransaction.java#L149-L162

With the JPA map store implementation a clear would detach all entities in the session, what is not intended here. Without the clear, Hibernate will only flush changes to the DB (as it sees that the modified entity is in a table that is being touched by the query), but will still keep the entity earmarked for an optimistic lock update at the end of the transaction to ensure that it hasn't been modified.


### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

After a bulk delete, the entities that are in the persistence context and that have been deleted by the bulk operation should also be marked as deleted in the persistence context.

### Actual behavior

Entities are still in the persistence context and might trigger a optimistic lock exception on commit.

### How to Reproduce?

Seen in #12241
commit_message:
Keep a list of model instances in the JPA map session.

This allows removing them from the persistence context on bulk delete.

Closes #12384

issue_title: KC_DB_USERNAME and KC_DB_PASSWORD are not respected when properties are set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When `db-username` and `db-password` properties are defined in the `/opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf` file and `KC_DB_USERNAME` and `KC_DB_PASSWORD` environment variables are set, Keycloak does not respect these environment variables and uses values from the file. However args `--db-username` and `--db-password` override properties from the file.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

`KC_DB_USERNAME` and `KC_DB_PASSWORD` environment variables should override `db-username` and `db-password` properties defined in the `/opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf` file

### Actual behavior

`db-username` and `db-password` properties are used instead of respectful environment variables.

### How to Reproduce?

Dockerfile:
```
ARG BASE_IMAGE_PATH
ARG BASE_IMAGE_VERSION

FROM ${BASE_IMAGE_PATH}:${BASE_IMAGE_VERSION}

ENV KC_DB=postgres

COPY conf/ /opt/keycloak/conf/
RUN /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build

```
conf/keycloak.conf file:
```
# Database settings
db=postgres
db-url-host=postgres
db-url-port=5432
db-url-database=keycloak
db-schema=public
db-username=keycloak
db-password=password

# Cache settings
cache=local

# Hostname settings
hostname-strict=false

# HTTP settings
http-enabled=true
http-relative-path=auth

# Proxy mode
proxy=edge

# Metrics and healthcheck settings
metrics-enabled=true
health-enabled=true

# Enabled features
features.admin_fine_grained_authz=enabled
features.client_secret_rotation=enabled
features.token_exchange=enabled

# Logging configuration
log-level=info
log-console-color=true

# Override for local development mode
%dev.spi-theme-cache-themes=false
%dev.spi-theme-cache-templates=false
%dev.spi-theme-static-max-age=-1

# Logging configuration. INFO is the default level for most of the categories
#quarkus.log.level = DEBUG
quarkus.log.category."org.jboss.resteasy.resteasy_jaxrs.i18n".level=WARN
quarkus.log.category."org.infinispan.transaction.lookup.JBossStandaloneJTAManagerLookup".level=WARN
quarkus.log.sentry=true
quarkus.log.sentry.dsn=https://sentry
quarkus.log.sentry.in-app-packages=*
quarkus.log.sentry.level=WARN
quarkus.log.sentry.server-name=keycloak
quarkus.log.sentry.release=18.0.0-iponweb-10.0
quarkus.log.sentry.environment=dev

docker build --build-arg BASE_IMAGE_PATH=quay.io/keycloak/keycloak --build-arg BASE_IMAGE_VERSION=18.0.0 . -t keycloak_test

It seems that Quarkus mixes tables from the two databases when Hibernate loads during startup and the "Realm" table overrides the default one ("REALM").
The strange thing is that sometimes no error appears during bootstrap, maybe because Keycloak entities classes were loaded first?

After a lot of investigations, I found that this error is not related to the UserStorageProvider implementation but is caused by the presence of a Realm class into the persistence.xml file.

I made a really simple demo with only a persistence.xml file embedded in a jar, with only a Realm class. No UserStorageProvider is needed to reproduce this error.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Using an external database with a table already defined in Keycloak does not throw an exception at startup, like in the Wildfly distribution.

### Actual behavior

Sometimes server does not start because the table Realm cannot be found by Hibernate.

### How to Reproduce?

Update keycloak.conf as follow:
```
db=mariadb
db-username=keycloak
db-password=keycloak
db-url=jdbc:mariadb://127.0.0.1:3306/keycloak
```

Add a quarkus.properties file with:
```
quarkus.datasource.user-store.db-kind=mariadb
quarkus.datasource.user-store.username=sa
quarkus.datasource.user-store.password=sa
quarkus.datasource.user-store.jdbc.url=jdbc:mariadb://127.0.0.1:3306/external_db
```

Create two empty databases "keycloak" and "external_db" in your MariaDB instance and also the corresponding users.

Copy the jar from the tar.gz archive attached into the providers directory and launch Keycloak with command:
`./kc.sh build && ./kc.sh start-dev --transaction-xa-enabled=false`

Note : it may require multiple launches to display the error.

[user-storage-jpa-realm.tar.gz](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/8870822/user-storage-jpa-realm.tar.gz)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: AuthzClient fails if it can not access the frontend-url
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using the AuthzClient in a backend server, which can only access the keycloak through the backend-url not the frontend-url fails. 

The problem is, that the .well-known/uma2-configuration returns the frontend-urls for the "policy_endpoint" and the "permission_endpoint".

We run into the problem when using quarkus integration tests, where the keycloak is run inside the same docker-network as the docker-container under test. So the quarkus-application can access the keycloak only through the backend-url, whereas the application running the unit-tests can only access through the front url, the port bound to localhost.

There should either be a possibility to get the uma2-configuration with backend-urls. Possibly return backend-url endpoints when accessing through the backend-url and return frontend-url when accessing through the frontend-url?

Or there should be a possibility to override the urls for the AuthzClient instead of using the well-known endpoint.


### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

The AuthzClient should also be working with backend-urls only

### Actual behavior

The AuthzClient fails if it has no access to the frontend-urls

### How to Reproduce?

* Configur a frontendurl for the 
* Call the https://keyclaok-backend-url/realms/{relam}/.well-known/uma2-configuration., then you get the "policy_endpoint" and as a frontent-url.

### Anything else?

_No response_
